API docs by Redocly

Mojo Helpdesk API (v2)

Download OpenAPI specification:

Welcome to the Mojo Helpdesk API reference docs. Whether you're an experienced developer and Mojo Helpdesk user or just starting out, this is where you'll find the documentation you need.

This API provides access to Mojo Helpdesk’s ticket management system, enabling the retrieval, creation, updating, and management of support tickets and related resources.

Getting Started

  • Get the API key from the Mojo Helpdesk account profile settings.
  • Use the following base URL for all requests: https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/
  • Include the access key as a query parameter in every request: ?access_key=
  • Call the endpoints described below to retrieve, create, update, and manage helpdesk data.

Python example

A Mojo Helpdesk example API usage Python script is available here.

Support

For API support, please contact https://help.mojohelpdesk.com

Tickets

List tickets

Retrieve a list of tickets from your helpdesk. This endpoint supports pagination and sorting.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
sort_by
string
Default: "id"
Enum: "id" "title" "description" "user_id" "assigned_to_id" "status_id" "ticket_form_id" "priority_id" "ticket_queue_id" "company_id" "rating" "rated_on" "created_on" "updated_on" "status_changed_on" "solved_on" "assigned_on" "ticket_type_id" "due_on" "scheduled_on"
sort_order
string
Default: "desc"
Enum: "asc" "desc"
page
integer
Default: 1
per_page
integer
Default: 10

Responses

/api/v2/tickets

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl -G 'https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets' \
  --data-urlencode 'access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>' \
  --data-urlencode 'sort_by=created_on' \
  --data-urlencode 'sort_order=desc'

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 123,
    • "title": "Printer is jammed",
    • "description": "The printer in the office is stuck",
    • "status_id": 50,
    • "ticket_queue_id": 1,
    • "ticket_form_id": 1,
    • "ticket_type_id": 2,
    • "priority_id": 30,
    • "user_id": 5,
    • "assigned_to_id": 10,
    • "company_id": 1,
    • "rating": 100,
    • "rated_on": "2024-01-16T09:00:00Z",
    • "rating_comment": "Fast and helpful resolution, thank you!",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_on": "2024-01-16T09:00:00Z",
    • "status_changed_on": "2024-01-16T08:45:00Z",
    • "solved_on": "2024-01-16T08:45:00Z",
    • "first_assigned_on": "2024-01-15T10:35:00Z",
    • "due_on": "2024-01-17T17:00:00Z",
    • "scheduled_on": null,
    • "closed_on": null,
    • "deleted_at": null,
    • "resolution_id": 1,
    • "created_from": 3,
    • "tag_ids": [
      • 1,
      • 2,
      • 3
      ],
    • "tags": [
      • {
        • "id": 1,
        • "label": "Bug",
        • "color": "#ff0000"
        }
      ],
    • "asset_tag": "ASSET-0001"
    }
]

Create a ticket

Create a new support ticket with optional file attachments. Use multipart/form-data content type to include file attachments.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
Request Body schema:
required
title
required
string

The title of the ticket

description
string

The description or body of the ticket

ticket_queue_id
required
integer

ID of the ticket queue to assign the ticket to

priority_id
integer
Enum: 10 20 30 40

Priority of the ticket, given as a numeric id:

  • 10 - Emergency
  • 20 - Urgent
  • 30 - Normal
  • 40 - Low
status_id
integer
Enum: 10 20 30 40 50 60

Status of the ticket, given as a numeric id:

  • 10 - New
  • 20 - In Progress
  • 30 - On Hold
  • 40 - Information Requested
  • 50 - Solved
  • 60 - Closed
ticket_type_id
integer

ID of the ticket type

assigned_to_id
integer

ID of the user assigned to the ticket

ticket_form_id
integer

ID of the ticket form to use (if omitted, the default form will be used)

user_id
integer

ID of the user who created the ticket

cc
string

Comma-separated list of email addresses to CC on ticket notifications

asset_tag
string

Asset tag to associate with the ticket

asset_id
integer

ID of the asset to associate with the ticket

due_on
string <date-time>

Due date for the ticket

scheduled_on
string <date-time>

Scheduled date for the ticket

resolution_id
integer

ID of the resolution to associate with the ticket

object

User information for the ticket

custom_field_<CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY>
string

Custom field value where CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY is the name of the custom field (e.g., custom_field_my_awesome_field)

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/tickets

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • curl with user
  • JavaScript with Attachments
  • Python
Content type
{
  • "title": "string",
  • "description": "string",
  • "ticket_queue_id": 0,
  • "priority_id": 10,
  • "status_id": 10,
  • "ticket_type_id": 0,
  • "assigned_to_id": 0,
  • "ticket_form_id": 0,
  • "user_id": 0,
  • "cc": "string",
  • "asset_tag": "string",
  • "asset_id": 0,
  • "due_on": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
  • "scheduled_on": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
  • "resolution_id": 0,
  • "user": {
    • "email": "user@example.com"
    },
  • "custom_field_<CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY>": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 401
  • 422
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 123,
  • "title": "Printer is jammed",
  • "description": "The printer in the office is stuck and won't print anything",
  • "status_id": 50,
  • "ticket_queue_id": 1,
  • "ticket_form_id": 1,
  • "ticket_type_id": 2,
  • "priority_id": 30,
  • "user_id": 5,
  • "assigned_to_id": 10,
  • "company_id": 1,
  • "rating": 100,
  • "rated_on": "2024-01-16T09:00:00Z",
  • "rating_comment": "Fast and helpful resolution, thank you!",
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_on": "2024-01-16T09:00:00Z",
  • "status_changed_on": "2024-01-16T08:45:00Z",
  • "solved_on": "2024-01-16T08:45:00Z",
  • "assigned_on": "2024-01-15T10:35:00Z",
  • "first_assigned_on": "2024-01-15T10:35:00Z",
  • "due_on": "2024-01-17T17:00:00Z",
  • "scheduled_on": null,
  • "created_from": 3,
  • "resolution_id": 1,
  • "parent_ticket_id": null,
  • "legacy_id": null,
  • "is_attention_required": false,
  • "user_attention_id": null,
  • "cc": "manager@company.com",
  • "time_to_first_comment": 1800,
  • "first_commented_on": "2024-01-15T11:00:00Z",
  • "resolution": {
    • "id": 1,
    • "name": "Fixed"
    },
  • "ticket_type": {
    • "id": 2,
    • "name": "Bug Report"
    },
  • "ticket_queue": {
    • "id": 1,
    • "name": "Technical Support",
    • "email_alias": "tech-support",
    • "email_forward": "tech@company.com",
    • "email_ticket_form_id": 1
    },
  • "tags": [
    • {
      • "id": 1,
      • "label": "Bug",
      • "color": "#ff0000"
      }
    ],
  • "custom_field_values": [
    • {
      • "name": "custom_field_asset_serial",
      • "value": "SN-00123"
      }
    ],
  • "tasks": [
    • {
      • "id": 777,
      • "title": "Check printer status",
      • "notes": "Verify if printer is online and has paper",
      • "is_completed": false,
      • "ticket_id": 123,
      • "assigned_to_id": 10,
      • "account_id": 1,
      • "due_on": "2024-01-16T17:00:00Z",
      • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      • "position": 0
      }
    ],
  • "all_comments": [
    • {
      • "id": 1,
      • "body": "This is a comment on the ticket",
      • "user_id": 10,
      • "ticket_id": 123,
      • "is_private": false,
      • "time_spent": 30,
      • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
      }
    ],
  • "staff_notes": [
    • {
      • "id": 1,
      • "body": "Internal note for staff only",
      • "user_id": 10,
      • "ticket_id": 123,
      • "time_spent": 15,
      • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
      }
    ],
  • "attachments": [
    • {
      • "id": 6422878,
      • "filename": "document.pdf",
      • "content_type": "application/pdf",
      • "content_size": 1024000,
      • "ticket_id": 123,
      • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      • "created_by_user_id": 10,
      • "comment_id": null,
      • "staff_note_id": null
      }
    ],
  • "staff_only_attachments": [ ],
  • "potential_assignees": [
    • {
      • "id": 10,
      • "name": "Jane Agent"
      }
    ],
  • "custom_field_types": [
    • {
      • "name": "custom_field_asset_serial",
      • "type": 1,
      • "type_name": "Text"
      }
    ],
  • "time_tracking_unit": {
    • "id": 1,
    • "name": "Minutes"
    },
  • "related_data": {
    • "ticket_queue": {
      • "id": 1,
      • "name": "Technical Support"
      }
    },
  • "asset_tag": "ASSET-0001"
}

Get a ticket

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl -G 'https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/123?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>'

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json

Update a ticket

PUT and PATCH are equivalent here - both apply a partial update, changing only the fields present in the request body.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer
Request Body schema: application/json
required
title
string

The title of the ticket

description
string

The description or body of the ticket

ticket_queue_id
integer

ID of the ticket queue to assign the ticket to

priority_id
integer
Enum: 10 20 30 40

Priority of the ticket, given as a numeric id:

  • 10 - Emergency
  • 20 - Urgent
  • 30 - Normal
  • 40 - Low
status_id
integer
Enum: 10 20 30 40 50 60

Status of the ticket, given as a numeric id:

  • 10 - New
  • 20 - In Progress
  • 30 - On Hold
  • 40 - Information Requested
  • 50 - Solved
  • 60 - Closed
ticket_type_id
integer

ID of the ticket type

assigned_to_id
integer

ID of the user assigned to the ticket

ticket_form_id
integer

ID of the ticket form to use (if omitted, the default form will be used)

user_id
integer

ID of the user who created the ticket

cc
string

Comma-separated list of email addresses to CC on ticket notifications

asset_tag
string

Asset tag to associate with the ticket

asset_id
integer

ID of the asset to associate with the ticket

due_on
string <date-time>

Due date for the ticket

scheduled_on
string <date-time>

Scheduled date for the ticket

resolution_id
integer

ID of the resolution to associate with the ticket

custom_field_<CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY>
string

Custom field value where CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY is the name of the custom field (e.g., custom_field_my_awesome_field)

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{id}

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "title": "string",
  • "description": "string",
  • "ticket_queue_id": 0,
  • "priority_id": 10,
  • "status_id": 10,
  • "ticket_type_id": 0,
  • "assigned_to_id": 0,
  • "ticket_form_id": 0,
  • "user_id": 0,
  • "cc": "string",
  • "asset_tag": "string",
  • "asset_id": 0,
  • "due_on": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
  • "scheduled_on": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
  • "resolution_id": 0,
  • "custom_field_<CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY>": "string"
}

Destroy ticket

Permanently delete a ticket from the system. This action cannot be undone. The ticket and all its associated data (comments, attachments, etc.) will be removed.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to delete

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/113?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X DELETE

Response samples

  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "message": "You are not authorized for that action.",
  • "messages": [
    • "You are not authorized for that action."
    ]
}

List events for a ticket

Retrieve a list of event log entries for a specific ticket. Events include actions like ticket creation, status changes, assignments, comments, and other activities performed on the ticket.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to get events for

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/events

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl -G 'https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/113/events?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>'

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 123,
    • "user_id": 10,
    • "user_full_name": "John Doe",
    • "unique_identifier": "event_123",
    • "entity_type": 1,
    • "entity_id": 456,
    • "action_name": "create",
    • "description": "Ticket created",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "picture_url": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg"
    }
]

Add tag to a ticket

Add a tag to a specific ticket. You can specify the tag either by its ID or by its label. If a tag with the specified label doesn't exist, it will be created.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to add the tag to

Request Body schema: application/json
required
tag_id
integer

ID of the existing tag to add

tag_label
string

Label of the tag to add (will create if doesn't exist)

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{id}/add_tag

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "tag_label": "Test"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1,
    • "label": "Bug",
    • "color": "#ff0000"
    }
]

Remove tag from a ticket

Remove a tag from a specific ticket. You can specify the tag either by its ID or by its label. The tag will be removed from the ticket if it exists.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to remove the tag from

Request Body schema: application/json
required
tag_id
integer

ID of the existing tag to remove

tag_label
string

Label of the tag to remove

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{id}/remove_tag

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "tag_label": "Test"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1,
    • "label": "Bug",
    • "color": "#ff0000"
    }
]

List comments for a ticket

Retrieve a list of comments for a specific ticket. Comments include user-submitted responses and internal notes associated with the ticket.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to get comments for

query Parameters
page
integer
Default: 1

Page number for pagination

per_page
integer
Default: 10

Number of comments per page

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/comments

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/114/comments?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1,
    • "body": "This is a comment on the ticket",
    • "user_id": 10,
    • "ticket_id": 123,
    • "is_private": false,
    • "time_spent": 30,
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "related_data": {
      • "user": {
        • "id": 10,
        • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
        }
      }
    }
]

Create comment on ticket

Add a new comment to a specific ticket. Comments can be public (visible to ticket submitter) or internal notes (visible only to agents).

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to add the comment to

Request Body schema: application/json
required
body
required
string

The content of the comment

time_spent
integer

Time spent on this comment in minutes

cc
string

Comma-separated list of email addresses to CC on this comment

user_id
integer

ID of the user to attribute the comment to. Must be a user in the same account; if omitted or not a valid account user, the comment is attributed to the caller.

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/comments

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "body": "New comment",
  • "time_spent": 30,
  • "cc": "user@example.com, manager@example.com",
  • "user_id": 10
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "body": "This is a comment on the ticket",
  • "user_id": 10,
  • "ticket_id": 123,
  • "is_private": false,
  • "time_spent": 30,
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "related_data": {
    • "user": {
      • "id": 10,
      • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
      }
    }
}

List staff notes for a ticket

Retrieve a list of staff notes for a specific ticket. Staff notes are internal comments visible only to agents and managers, not to the ticket submitter.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to get staff notes for

query Parameters
page
integer
Default: 1

Page number for pagination

per_page
integer
Default: 10

Number of staff notes per page

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/staff_notes

  • 404

application/json

    • "ticket_id": 123,

    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",

        • "full_name": "Jane Agent"

Create staff note on ticket

Add a new staff note to a specific ticket. Staff notes are internal comments visible only to agents and managers, not to the ticket submitter.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to add the staff note to

Request Body schema: application/json
required
body
required
string

The content of the staff note

time_spent
integer

Time spent on this staff note in minutes

cc
string

Comma-separated list of email addresses to CC on this staff note

user_id
integer

ID of the user to attribute the staff note to. Must be a user in the same account; if omitted or not a valid account user, the staff note is attributed to the caller.

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/staff_notes

Request samples

  • Python
Content type
application/json

  • "cc": "manager@example.com",
  • "user_id": 10
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "body": "Internal note for staff only",
  • "user_id": 10,
  • "ticket_id": 123,
  • "time_spent": 15,
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "related_data": {
    • "user": {
      • "id": 10,
      • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
      }
    }
}

Search tickets

Search for tickets using ElasticSearch query syntax. The search query format is the same as the one generated for the advanced search on the web interface.

Important: When using special characters in the query, use URL encoding:

  • Use %20 instead of space
  • Use \& instead of just &
  • Use \( instead of (
  • Use \< instead of <

Example Queries:

  • All open tickets: query=status.id:\(\<50\)
  • All urgent open tickets: query=priority.id:\(\<=20\)%20AND%20status.id:\(\<50\)
  • All open tickets in certain queue: query=ticket_queue.id:19647%20AND%20status.id:\(\<50\)
  • All tickets with due date: query=_exists_:due_on

Searchable Fields:

  • assignee.id, assignee.email
  • comments.body
  • company.id
  • created_by.id, created_by.name, created_by.email
  • created_on
  • custom_fields
  • description
  • due_on
  • priority.id
  • ticket_queue.id
  • rating
  • rated_on
  • scheduled_on
  • solved_on
  • status.id
  • status_changed_on
  • tags.id
  • ticket_type.id
  • ticket_form.id
  • title
  • updated_on
Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
query
string

ElasticSearch query in the format used by the web interface advanced search. If not provided, returns all tickets.

Search Notes:

  • Format of all date fields is: 2013-11-11T21:37:02Z
  • To search for range of date/time (i.e. for created_on field):
    • created_on:[2013-11-11T21:37:02Z TO *] (for dates after the given)
    • created_on:[* TO 2013-11-11T21:37:02Z] (for dates before the given)
    • created_on:[2013-10-11T21:37:02Z TO 2013-11-11T21:37:02Z] (for dates between the given)
  • Surround all string values with parentheses and double quotes like the following examples:
    • created_by.email:("myemail@somedomain.com")
sf
string
Enum: "created_on" "due_on" "rated_on" "scheduled_on" "solved_on" "updated_on"
r
integer
Default: 0
Enum: 0 1

Reverse sort (0 = ascending, 1 = descending)

per_page
integer >= 10
Default: 10

Results per page (minimum 10)

page
integer
Default: 1

Page number

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/search

Request samples

  • curl - All open tickets
  • curl - All urgent open tickets
  • curl - All tickets with due date
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/search?query=status.id:\(\<50\)\&sf=created_on\&r=0\&access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 123,
    • "title": "Printer is jammed",
    • "description": "The printer in the office is stuck",
    • "status_id": 50,
    • "ticket_queue_id": 1,
    • "ticket_form_id": 1,
    • "ticket_type_id": 2,
    • "priority_id": 30,
    • "user_id": 5,
    • "assigned_to_id": 10,
    • "company_id": 1,
    • "rating": 100,
    • "rated_on": "2024-01-16T09:00:00Z",
    • "rating_comment": "Fast and helpful resolution, thank you!",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_on": "2024-01-16T09:00:00Z",
    • "status_changed_on": "2024-01-16T08:45:00Z",
    • "solved_on": "2024-01-16T08:45:00Z",
    • "first_assigned_on": "2024-01-15T10:35:00Z",
    • "due_on": "2024-01-17T17:00:00Z",
    • "scheduled_on": null,
    • "closed_on": null,
    • "deleted_at": null,
    • "resolution_id": 1,
    • "created_from": 3,
    • "tag_ids": [
      • 1,
      • 2,
      • 3
      ],
    • "tags": [
      • {
        • "id": 1,
        • "label": "Bug",
        • "color": "#ff0000"
        }
      ],
    • "asset_tag": "ASSET-0001"
    }
]

Events

List events for a ticket

Retrieve a list of event log entries for a specific ticket. Events include actions like ticket creation, status changes, assignments, comments, and other activities performed on the ticket.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to get events for

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/events

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl -G 'https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/113/events?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>'

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 123,
    • "user_id": 10,
    • "user_full_name": "John Doe",
    • "unique_identifier": "event_123",
    • "entity_type": 1,
    • "entity_id": 456,
    • "action_name": "create",
    • "description": "Ticket created",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "picture_url": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg"
    }
]

List event log entries

Retrieve a paginated list of event log entries for the account, ordered by creation time (newest first). Optionally filter to a single ticket by passing ticket_id. Requires the Manager role.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
page
integer
Default: 1

Page number for pagination.

per_page
integer <= 30
Default: 30

Number of entries per page. Capped at 30 (values above 30 are clamped to 30).

ticket_id
integer

When supplied, returns only the event log entries for that ticket.

Responses

/api/v2/events

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl -G 'https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/events?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>'

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 123,
    • "user_id": 10,
    • "user_full_name": "John Doe",
    • "unique_identifier": "event_123",
    • "entity_type": 1,
    • "entity_id": 456,
    • "action_name": "create",
    • "description": "Ticket created",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "picture_url": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg"
    }
]

Get an event log entry

Retrieve a single event log entry by its ID. Requires the Manager role.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

The ID of the event log entry.

Responses

/api/v2/events/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl -G 'https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/events/123?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>'

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 123,
  • "user_id": 10,
  • "user_full_name": "John Doe",
  • "unique_identifier": "event_123",
  • "entity_type": 1,
  • "entity_id": 456,
  • "action_name": "create",
  • "description": "Ticket created",
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "picture_url": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg"
}

Add tag to a ticket

Add a tag to a specific ticket. You can specify the tag either by its ID or by its label. If a tag with the specified label doesn't exist, it will be created.

path Parameters
id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to add the tag to

Request Body schema: application/json
required
tag_id
integer

ID of the existing tag to add

tag_label
string

Label of the tag to add (will create if doesn't exist)

List ticket tags

Retrieve all ticket tags for the account. This endpoint does NOT paginate - it returns the full list. Results can be ordered with sort_by and sort_order.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
sort_by
string
Default: "id"

Column to order by (e.g. id, label, color). Defaults to id.

sort_order
string
Default: "asc"
Enum: "asc" "desc"

Sort direction. Defaults to asc.

Responses

/api/v2/tags

Request samples

  • curl
  • curl with sorting
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tags?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1,
    • "label": "Bug",
    • "color": "#ff0000"
    }
]

Create tag

Create a new ticket tag.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
Request Body schema: application/json
required
label
required
string

Display name of the tag

color
required
string

Color code for the tag (hex format, e.g.,

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/tags

Content type
application/json

Update tag

Update an existing tag.

PUT and PATCH are equivalent here - both apply a partial update, changing only the fields present in the request body.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the tag

Request Body schema: application/json
required
label
string

Display name of the tag

color
string

Color code for the tag (hex format, e.g.,

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/tags/{id}

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "label": "Updated Tag",
  • "color": "#ff0000"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "label": "Bug",
  • "color": "#ff0000"
}

Destroy tag

Delete a tag. This action cannot be undone.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the tag

Responses

/api/v2/tags/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tags/10?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X DELETE

Response samples

  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "error": "Invalid company id"
}

Merge tag

Merge this tag into another tag. All tickets carrying this tag are re-tagged with the target tag (with_tag_id) and this tag is removed. Both the source tag (id) and the target tag (with_tag_id) must exist in the caller's account.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the source tag being merged (and removed)

Request Body schema: application/json
required
with_tag_id
required
integer

ID of the target tag this tag is merged into

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/tags/{id}/merge

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "with_tag_id": 12
}

Response samples

  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "error": "Invalid company id"
}

Retrieve a list of staff notes for a specific ticket. Staff notes are internal comments visible only to agents and managers, not to the ticket submitter.

Create staff note on ticket

Add a new staff note to a specific ticket. Staff notes are internal comments visible only to agents and managers, not to the ticket submitter.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

The ID of the ticket to add the staff note to

Request Body schema: application/json
required
body
required
string

The content of the staff note

time_spent
integer

Time spent on this staff note in minutes

cc
string

Comma-separated list of email addresses to CC on this staff note

user_id
integer

ID of the user to attribute the staff note to. Must be a user in the same account; if omitted or not a valid account user, the staff note is attributed to the caller.

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/staff_notes

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "body": "New staff note",
  • "time_spent": 30,
  • "cc": "manager@example.com",
  • "user_id": 10
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "body": "Internal note for staff only",
  • "user_id": 10,
  • "ticket_id": 123,
  • "time_spent": 15,
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "related_data": {
    • "user": {
      • "id": 10,
      • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
      }
    }
}

Attachments

List attachments for a ticket

Retrieve all attachments associated with a specific ticket.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

ID of the ticket

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/attachments

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/211402/attachments?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 6422878,
    • "filename": "document.pdf",
    • "content_type": "application/pdf",
    • "content_size": 1024000,
    • "ticket_id": 211402,
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "created_by_user_id": 10,
    • "comment_id": null,
    • "staff_note_id": null
    }
]

Add attachment to a ticket, comment, or staff note

Upload a file attachment. Use multipart/form-data content type.

By default the file is attached to the ticket itself and is displayed alongside the ticket's original message. To make the file appear under a specific agent reply or internal note, first create the comment/staff note, then upload the attachment with comment_id or staff_note_id.

When more than one target is supplied, precedence is comment_id > staff_note_id > staff_only > ticket. The referenced comment or staff note must belong to the ticket in the path.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

ID of the ticket

query Parameters
staff_only
boolean
Default: false

When true (and no comment_id/staff_note_id is given), a new staff note is created to carry the file, so it is only visible to staff.

comment_id
integer

Attach the file to this existing comment (agent reply) so it appears under that reply. Must be a comment on the ticket in the path.

staff_note_id
integer

Attach the file to this existing staff note. Must be a staff note on the ticket in the path.

Request Body schema: multipart/form-data
required
file
string <binary>

The file to upload

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/attachments

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl -F "file=@/home/user/my-file.txt" https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/211402/attachments?staff_only=true&access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X POST

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 404
  • 422
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 6422878,
  • "filename": "document.pdf",
  • "content_type": "application/pdf",
  • "content_size": 1024000,
  • "ticket_id": 211402,
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "created_by_user_id": 10,
  • "comment_id": null,
  • "staff_note_id": null
}

Download an attachment

Download a specific attachment by its ID. This endpoint does NOT return the file bytes directly: it responds with a 302 redirect to a (temporary, signed) blob URL where the file can be downloaded. HTTP clients that follow redirects automatically will retrieve the file.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the attachment

Responses

/api/v2/attachments/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl -L https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/attachments/6422878?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "message": "You are not authorized for that action.",
  • "messages": [
    • "You are not authorized for that action."
    ]
}

Delete an attachment

Delete a specific attachment by its ID.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the attachment

Responses

/api/v2/attachments/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/attachments/6422878?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X DELETE

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 6422878,
  • "filename": "document.pdf",
  • "content_type": "application/pdf",
  • "content_size": 1024000,
  • "ticket_id": 211402,
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "created_by_user_id": 10,
  • "comment_id": null,
  • "staff_note_id": null
}

Ticket Queues

List ticket queues

Retrieve a list of ticket queues. This endpoint supports pagination.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
page
integer
Default: 1

Page number

per_page
integer
Default: 30

Results per page (default 30)

Responses

/api/v2/ticket_queues

Request samples

  • curl
  • curl with pagination
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/ticket_queues?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1,
    • "name": "Technical Support",
    • "email_alias": "tech-support",
    • "email_forward": "tech@company.com",
    • "email_ticket_form_id": 1
    }
]

Create ticket queue

Create a new ticket queue.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
Request Body schema: application/json
required
name
required
string

Name of the ticket queue

email_alias
string

Email alias for the queue

email_forward
string

Email address to forward queue emails to

email_ticket_form_id
integer or null

ID of the ticket form applied to tickets created from emails sent to this queue

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/ticket_queues

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "name": "My queue",
  • "email_alias": "support",
  • "email_forward": "support@company.com"
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "name": "Technical Support",
  • "email_alias": "tech-support",
  • "email_forward": "tech@company.com",
  • "email_ticket_form_id": 1
}

Show ticket queue

Retrieve details of a specific ticket queue.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the ticket queue

Responses

/api/v2/ticket_queues/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/ticket_queues/8?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "name": "Technical Support",
  • "email_alias": "tech-support",
  • "email_forward": "tech@company.com",
  • "email_ticket_form_id": 1
}

Update ticket queue

Update an existing ticket queue.

PUT and PATCH are equivalent here - both apply a partial update, changing only the fields present in the request body.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the ticket queue

Request Body schema: application/json
required
name
string

Name of the ticket queue

email_alias
string

Email alias for the queue

email_forward
string

Email address to forward queue emails to

email_ticket_form_id
integer or null

ID of the ticket form applied to tickets created from emails sent to this queue

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/ticket_queues/{id}

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "name": "My precious queue",
  • "email_alias": "premium-support",
  • "email_forward": "premium@company.com"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "name": "Technical Support",
  • "email_alias": "tech-support",
  • "email_forward": "tech@company.com",
  • "email_ticket_form_id": 1
}

Destroy ticket queue

Delete a ticket queue. This action cannot be undone.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the ticket queue

Responses

/api/v2/ticket_queues/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/ticket_queues/10?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X DELETE

Response samples

  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "error": "Invalid company id"
}

Groups

List groups

Retrieve a list of groups (formerly called companies). This endpoint supports pagination.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
page
integer
Default: 1

Page number

per_page
integer
Default: 30

Results per page (default 30)

Responses

/api/v2/groups

Request samples

  • curl
  • curl with pagination
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/groups?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1999,
    • "name": "My very own group",
    • "primary_contact_id": 10,
    • "billing_contact_id": 11,
    • "support_level_id": 1,
    • "support_status_id": 0,
    • "support_start_date": "2024-01-01",
    • "support_end_date": "2024-12-31",
    • "support_info_url": "https://support.company.com",
    • "address": "123 Main St",
    • "address2": "Suite 100",
    • "city": "New York",
    • "state": "NY",
    • "zip": "10001",
    • "country": "USA",
    • "website_url": "www.google.com",
    • "notes": "Premium customer"
    }
]

Create group

Create a new group (formerly called company).

Authorizations:
AccessKey
Request Body schema: application/json
required
name
required
string

Name of the group

primary_contact_id
integer

ID of the primary contact (existing helpdesk user)

billing_contact_id
integer

ID of the billing contact (existing helpdesk user)

support_level_id
integer

Support level ID

support_status_id
integer
Enum: 0 1

Support status (0 - active, 1 - delinquent)

support_start_date
string <date>

Support start date

support_end_date
string <date>

Support end date

support_info_url
string

URL for support information

address
string

Primary address

address2
string

Secondary address

city
string

City

state
string

State/Province

zip
string

ZIP/Postal code

country
string

Country

website_url
string

Website URL

notes
string

Additional notes

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/groups

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "name": "My very own group",
  • "primary_contact_id": 10,
  • "website_url": "www.company.com"
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1999,
  • "name": "My very own group",
  • "primary_contact_id": 10,
  • "billing_contact_id": 11,
  • "support_level_id": 1,
  • "support_status_id": 0,
  • "support_start_date": "2024-01-01",
  • "support_end_date": "2024-12-31",

  • "city": "New York",
  • "state": "NY",
  • "zip": "10001",
  • "country": "USA",
  • "website_url": "www.google.com",
  • "notes": "Premium customer"
}

integer

Users

List users

Retrieve a list of users. This endpoint supports pagination.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
page
integer
Default: 1

Page number

per_page
integer
Default: 30

Results per page (default 30)

Responses

/api/v2/users

Request samples

  • curl
  • curl with pagination
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/users?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1,
    • "email": "john.doe@company.com",
    • "first_name": "John",
    • "middle_name": "Q",
    • "last_name": "Doe",
    • "full_name": "John Doe",
    • "work_phone": "+1-555-123-4567",
    • "cell_phone": "+1-555-987-6543",
    • "home_phone": "+1-555-111-2222",
    • "fax_phone": null,
    • "address": "123 Main St, Springfield",
    • "user_notes": "Premium customer",
    • "helpdesk_notes": "VIP - respond within 1 hour",
    • "company_id": 1,
    • "role_id": 20,
    • "is_active": true,
    • "can_access_company_tickets": true,
    • "im_screen_name": null,
    • "last_login_on": "2024-01-16T08:00:00Z",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "last_active_on": "2024-01-16T09:15:00Z",
    • "helpdesk_name": "john-doe",
    • "organization_name": "Acme Corp",
    • "unassigned_tickets_count": 0,
    • "default_private_messages": false,
    • "do_not_email_ever": false,
    • "timer_auto_start": false,
    • "is_alpha_tester": false,
    • "is_beta_tester": false,
    • "time_zone": "Eastern Time (US & Canada)",
    • "picture_url": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg",
    • "access_key": "a1b2c3d4e5f6",
    • "is_on_vacation": false,
    • "is_asset_manager": false,
    • "related_data": {
      • "company": {
        • "id": 1,
        • "name": "Acme Corp"
        }
      }
    }
]

Create a user

Create a new user.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
send_welcome_email
integer
Enum: 0 1

Controls the welcome / invitation email sent to the new user. A welcome email is sent by default. Pass 0 to suppress it; omitting the parameter (or passing 1) sends the email.

Request Body schema: application/json
required
email
required
string <email> [ 6 .. 255 ] characters

Email address of the user. Must be unique within the account, between 6 and 255 characters, and not on the account's blocked/forbidden lists.

first_name
string <= 45 characters

First name of the user (maximum 45 characters)

last_name
string <= 45 characters

Last name of the user (maximum 45 characters)

work_phone
string <= 30 characters

Work phone number (maximum 30 characters)

cell_phone
string <= 30 characters

Cell phone number (maximum 30 characters)

home_phone
string <= 30 characters

Home phone number (maximum 30 characters)

user_notes
string

Additional notes about the user

company_id
integer

ID of the company/group the user belongs to. Must reference a company that exists in your account; an unknown id is rejected with 400 Invalid company id. Defaults to the account's default company when omitted.

password
string

Password for the user account. When omitted, a random compliant password is generated. When supplied, it must satisfy the account's security policy (by default at least 8 characters; the policy can also require minimum unique characters, digits, and uppercase characters, and block common passwords).

is_active
boolean

Whether the user account is active

custom_field_<CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY>
string

Custom field value where CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY is the name of the custom field (e.g., custom_field_employee_id)

role_id
integer
Default: 10
Enum: 10 15 20 30 35 40

Role of the new user, given as a numeric id. Defaults to 10 (regular user) when omitted. Higher numbers grant broader permissions:

role_id Role Description
10 Regular user End user / requester who can submit tickets (the default).
15 Restricted technician Legacy restricted agent, kept for backward compatibility.
20 Technician Agent who works tickets.
30 Manager Agent with management permissions.
35 Administrator Full account administration.
40 Account owner The account owner.

Constraints:

  • Cannot be higher than the calling user's own role (400 You are not allowed to set higher than your own role.).
  • Cannot exceed 40 / Owner (400 You are not allowed to set a super admin role.).
  • Any technician/agent role (anything above 10) requires a plan that permits agents and an available agent seat, otherwise the request is rejected with a plan-limit error.
property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/users

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "email": "john@example.com",
  • "first_name": "John",
  • "last_name": "Doe",
  • "company_id": "888",
  • "password": "Sup3rSecret"
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 400
  • 401
  • 422
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "email": "john.doe@company.com",
  • "first_name": "John",
  • "middle_name": "Q",
  • "last_name": "Doe",
  • "full_name": "John Doe",
  • "work_phone": "+1-555-123-4567",
  • "cell_phone": "+1-555-987-6543",
  • "home_phone": "+1-555-111-2222",
  • "fax_phone": null,
  • "address": "123 Main St, Springfield",
  • "user_notes": "Premium customer",
  • "helpdesk_notes": "VIP - respond within 1 hour",
  • "company_id": 1,
  • "role_id": 20,
  • "is_active": true,
  • "can_access_company_tickets": true,
  • "im_screen_name": null,
  • "last_login_on": "2024-01-16T08:00:00Z",
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "last_active_on": "2024-01-16T09:15:00Z",
  • "helpdesk_name": "john-doe",
  • "organization_name": "Acme Corp",
  • "unassigned_tickets_count": 0,
  • "default_private_messages": false,
  • "do_not_email_ever": false,
  • "timer_auto_start": false,
  • "is_alpha_tester": false,
  • "is_beta_tester": false,
  • "time_zone": "Eastern Time (US & Canada)",
  • "picture_url": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg",
  • "access_key": "a1b2c3d4e5f6",
  • "is_on_vacation": false,
  • "is_asset_manager": false,
  • "related_data": {
    • "company": {
      • "id": 1,
      • "name": "Acme Corp"
      }
    }
}

Authorizations:
AccessKey

Responses

  • 200
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1,

    • "first_name": "John",
    • "middle_name": "Q",
    • "last_name": "Doe",

    • "last_login_on": "2024-01-16T08:00:00Z",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "last_active_on": "2024-01-16T09:15:00Z",

    • "organization_name": "Acme Corp",
    • "unassigned_tickets_count": 0,
    • "default_private_messages": false,
    • "do_not_email_ever": false,
    • "timer_auto_start": false,
    • "is_alpha_tester": false,
    • "is_beta_tester": false,
    • "time_zone": "Eastern Time (US & Canada)",
    • "picture_url": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg",
    • "access_key": "a1b2c3d4e5f6",
    • "is_on_vacation": false,
    • "is_asset_manager": false,
    • "related_data": {
      • "company": {
        • "id": 1,
        • "name": "Acme Corp"
        }
      }
    }
]

Show user

Retrieve details of a specific user.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the user

Responses

/api/v2/users/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/users/1?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "email": "john.doe@company.com",
  • "first_name": "John",
  • "middle_name": "Q",
  • "last_name": "Doe",
  • "full_name": "John Doe",
  • "work_phone": "+1-555-123-4567",
  • "cell_phone": "+1-555-987-6543",
  • "home_phone": "+1-555-111-2222",
  • "fax_phone": null,
  • "address": "123 Main St, Springfield",
  • "user_notes": "Premium customer",
  • "helpdesk_notes": "VIP - respond within 1 hour",
  • "company_id": 1,
  • "role_id": 20,
  • "is_active": true,
  • "can_access_company_tickets": true,
  • "im_screen_name": null,
  • "last_login_on": "2024-01-16T08:00:00Z",
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "last_active_on": "2024-01-16T09:15:00Z",
  • "helpdesk_name": "john-doe",
  • "organization_name": "Acme Corp",
  • "unassigned_tickets_count": 0,
  • "default_private_messages": false,
  • "do_not_email_ever": false,
  • "timer_auto_start": false,
  • "is_alpha_tester": false,
  • "is_beta_tester": false,
  • "time_zone": "Eastern Time (US & Canada)",
  • "picture_url": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg",
  • "access_key": "a1b2c3d4e5f6",
  • "is_on_vacation": false,
  • "is_asset_manager": false,
  • "related_data": {
    • "company": {
      • "id": 1,
      • "name": "Acme Corp"
      }
    }
}

Update user

Update an existing user. This is a partial update - only the fields you include in the request body are changed; omitted fields are left untouched.

Both PUT and PATCH are accepted at this path and behave identically.

Note: email cannot be changed here (see UserUpdateRequest).

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the user

Request Body schema: application/json
required
first_name
string

First name of the user

last_name
string

Last name of the user

work_phone
string

Work phone number

cell_phone
string

Cell phone number

home_phone
string

Home phone number

user_notes
string

Additional notes about the user

company_id
integer

ID of the company/group the user belongs to

password
string

Password for the user account

is_active
boolean

Whether the user account is active

custom_field_<CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY>
string

Custom field value where CUSTOM_FIELD_SYSTEM_KEY is the name of the custom field (e.g., custom_field_employee_id)

role_id
integer
Enum: 10 15 20 30 35 40

New role for the user, given as a numeric id. Higher numbers grant broader permissions:

role_id Role Description
10 Regular user End user / requester who can submit tickets.
15 Restricted technician Legacy restricted agent, kept for backward compatibility.
20 Technician Agent who works tickets.
30 Manager Agent with management permissions.
35 Administrator Full account administration.
40 Account owner The account owner.

You cannot raise a user's role above your own, and you cannot update a user whose current role is higher than yours.

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/users/{id}

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "user_notes": "Thats me again.",
  • "first_name": "Updated Name",
  • "role_id": 20
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 422
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "email": "john.doe@company.com",
  • "first_name": "John",
  • "middle_name": "Q",
  • "last_name": "Doe",
  • "full_name": "John Doe",
  • "work_phone": "+1-555-123-4567",
  • "cell_phone": "+1-555-987-6543",
  • "home_phone": "+1-555-111-2222",
  • "fax_phone": null,
  • "address": "123 Main St, Springfield",
  • "user_notes": "Premium customer",
  • "helpdesk_notes": "VIP - respond within 1 hour",
  • "company_id": 1,
  • "role_id": 20,
  • "is_active": true,
  • "can_access_company_tickets": true,
  • "im_screen_name": null,
  • "last_login_on": "2024-01-16T08:00:00Z",
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "last_active_on": "2024-01-16T09:15:00Z",
  • "helpdesk_name": "john-doe",
  • "organization_name": "Acme Corp",
  • "unassigned_tickets_count": 0,
  • "default_private_messages": false,
  • "do_not_email_ever": false,
  • "timer_auto_start": false,
  • "is_alpha_tester": false,
  • "is_beta_tester": false,
  • "time_zone": "Eastern Time (US & Canada)",
  • "picture_url": "https://example.com/avatar.jpg",
  • "access_key": "a1b2c3d4e5f6",
  • "is_on_vacation": false,
  • "is_asset_manager": false,
  • "related_data": {
    • "company": {
      • "id": 1,
      • "name": "Acme Corp"
      }
    }
}

Destroy user

Delete a user. This action cannot be undone.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the user

Responses

/api/v2/users/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/users/1999?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X DELETE

Response samples

  • 401
  • 404
  • 422
Content type
application/json
{
  • "message": "You are not authorized for that action.",
  • "messages": [
    • "You are not authorized for that action."
    ]
}

AccessKey
query Parameters
email
required
string <email>

Tasks

List ticket tasks

Retrieve a list of tasks associated with a specific ticket.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

ID of the ticket

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/tasks

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/88/tasks?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 777,
    • "title": "Check printer status",
    • "notes": "Verify if printer is online and has paper",
    • "is_completed": false,
    • "ticket_id": 88,
    • "assigned_to_id": 10,
    • "account_id": 1,
    • "due_on": "2024-01-16T17:00:00Z",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "position": 0,
    • "assignee": {
      • "id": 10,
      • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
      }
    }
]

Create ticket task

Create a new task for a specific ticket.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

ID of the ticket

Request Body schema: application/json
required
title
required
string

Title of the task

notes
string

Additional notes for the task

is_completed
boolean

Whether the task is completed

due_on
string <date-time>

Due date for the task

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/tasks

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "title": "Test",
  • "notes": "Additional task notes",
  • "is_completed": false
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 400
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 777,
  • "title": "Check printer status",
  • "notes": "Verify if printer is online and has paper",
  • "is_completed": false,
  • "ticket_id": 88,
  • "assigned_to_id": 10,
  • "account_id": 1,
  • "due_on": "2024-01-16T17:00:00Z",
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "position": 0,
  • "assignee": {
    • "id": 10,
    • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
    }
}

Update ticket task

Update an existing task for a specific ticket.

PUT and PATCH are equivalent here - both apply a partial update, changing only the fields present in the request body.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

ID of the ticket

id
required
integer

ID of the task

Request Body schema: application/json
required
title
string

Title of the task

notes
string

Additional notes for the task

is_completed
boolean

Whether the task is completed

due_on
string <date-time>

Due date for the task

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/tasks/{id}

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "notes": "Help",
  • "is_completed": true,
  • "title": "Updated task title"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 777,
  • "title": "Check printer status",
  • "notes": "Verify if printer is online and has paper",
  • "is_completed": false,
  • "ticket_id": 88,
  • "assigned_to_id": 10,
  • "account_id": 1,
  • "due_on": "2024-01-16T17:00:00Z",
  • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "position": 0,
  • "assignee": {
    • "id": 10,
    • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
    }
}

Destroy ticket task

Delete a task from a specific ticket. This action cannot be undone.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

ID of the ticket

id
required
integer

ID of the task

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/tasks/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/88/tasks/777?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X DELETE

Response samples

  • 400
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "error": "Invalid company id"
}

Reorder ticket tasks

Set the display order of a ticket's tasks. Provide the task ids in the desired order via the keys array; each task's position is updated to match its index in the array (ids that do not belong to the ticket are ignored). Returns the ticket's tasks after reordering.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

ID of the ticket

Request Body schema: application/json
required
keys
required
Array of integers

Task ids in the desired order

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/tasks/reorder

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "keys": [
    • 777,
    • 778,
    • 779
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 777,
    • "title": "Check printer status",
    • "notes": "Verify if printer is online and has paper",
    • "is_completed": false,
    • "ticket_id": 88,
    • "assigned_to_id": 10,
    • "account_id": 1,
    • "due_on": "2024-01-16T17:00:00Z",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "position": 0,
    • "assignee": {
      • "id": 10,
      • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
      }
    }
]

Delete completed ticket tasks

Delete all completed tasks on a ticket. Returns the ticket's remaining (uncompleted) tasks.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
ticket_id
required
integer

ID of the ticket

Responses

/api/v2/tickets/{ticket_id}/tasks/delete_completed

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/tickets/88/tasks/delete_completed?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X POST

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 777,
    • "title": "Check printer status",
    • "notes": "Verify if printer is online and has paper",
    • "is_completed": false,
    • "ticket_id": 88,
    • "assigned_to_id": 10,
    • "account_id": 1,
    • "due_on": "2024-01-16T17:00:00Z",
    • "created_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_on": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "position": 0,
    • "assignee": {
      • "id": 10,
      • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
      }
    }
]

Ticket Forms

List ticket forms

Retrieve a list of ticket forms.

Note: unlike ticket queues and ticket types (whose read actions allow Agents), every ticket form action - including this read - requires the Manager role.

Authorizations:
AccessKey

Responses

/api/v2/ticket_forms

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/ticket_forms?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 2700,
    • "name": "Default Form",
    • "is_default": true,
    • "is_enabled": true,
    • "description": "Default ticket form",
    • "is_visible_for_end_user": true,
    • "show_cc": true,
    • "show_attach_files": true,
    • "is_visible_for_non_logged_in_user": false,
    • "hidden_field_attribute_ids": [
      • 101,
      • 102
      ],
    • "field_attributes": [
      • {
        • "id": 201,
        • "name": "priority_id",
        • "label": "Priority",
        • "is_required": true
        }
      ],
    • "form_field_rules": [
      • {
        • "id": 301,
        • "field_attribute_id": 201,
        • "condition": "equals",
        • "value": "10"
        }
      ],
    • "available_rule_conditions": {
      • "1": [
        • {
          • "name": "equals",
          • "label": "Equals",
          • "require_value": true
          }
        ]
      }
    }
]

Show ticket form

Retrieve details of a specific ticket form.

Note: unlike ticket queues and ticket types (whose read actions allow Agents), every ticket form action - including this read - requires the Manager role.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the ticket form

Responses

/api/v2/ticket_forms/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/ticket_forms/2700?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 2700,
  • "name": "Default Form",
  • "is_default": true,
  • "is_enabled": true,
  • "description": "Default ticket form",
  • "is_visible_for_end_user": true,
  • "show_cc": true,
  • "show_attach_files": true,
  • "is_visible_for_non_logged_in_user": false,
  • "hidden_field_attribute_ids": [
    • 101,
    • 102
    ],
  • "field_attributes": [
    • {
      • "id": 201,
      • "name": "priority_id",
      • "label": "Priority",
      • "is_required": true
      }
    ],
  • "form_field_rules": [
    • {
      • "id": 301,
      • "field_attribute_id": 201,
      • "condition": "equals",
      • "value": "10"
      }
    ],
  • "available_rule_conditions": {
    • "1": [
      • {
        • "name": "equals",
        • "label": "Equals",
        • "require_value": true
        }
      ]
    }
}

Group Access

List all groups tickets access for a user

Retrieve all group ticket access permissions for a specific user.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
user_id
required
integer

ID of the user

Responses

/api/v2/users/{user_id}/group_access

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/users/14/group_access?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "user_id": 14,
    • "group_id": 1234,
    • "access": 1
    }
]

Get single group tickets access for a user

Retrieve the ticket access permission for a specific user and group.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
user_id
required
integer

ID of the user

group_id
required
integer

ID of the group

Responses

/api/v2/users/{user_id}/group_access/{group_id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/users/14/group_access/1234?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "user_id": 14,
  • "group_id": 1234,
  • "access": 1
}

Set single group tickets access for a user

Set the ticket access permission for a specific user and group.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
user_id
required
integer

ID of the user

group_id
required
integer

ID of the group

Request Body schema: application/json
required
access
required
integer
Enum: 0 1 2

Access level the user has to the group's (company's) tickets:

  • 0 - No access
  • 1 - Full access (view and comment on the group's tickets)
  • 2 - Comment only (view and comment, but not full access)
property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/users/{user_id}/group_access/{group_id}

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "access": 1
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
Content type
application/json
{
  • "user_id": 14,
  • "group_id": 1234,
  • "access": 1
}

Assets

List assets

Retrieve a list of assets.

Authorizations:
AccessKey

Responses

/api/v2/assets

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/assets?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 403
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1,
    • "asset_tag": "PRINT-001",
    • "serial_number": "SN123456789",
    • "legacy_reference_number": null,
    • "display_name": "Office Printer HP LaserJet",
    • "description": "My very own asset",
    • "asset_type_id": 1,
    • "location_id": 1,
    • "department_id": 1,
    • "managed_by_id": 10,
    • "used_by_id": 15,
    • "asset_status_id": 1,
    • "notes": "Located in main office",
    • "purchased_on": "2024-01-01",
    • "cost": 299.99,
    • "purchase_order_number": "PO-2024-001",
    • "vendor": "HP Inc.",
    • "replaced_on": null,
    • "warranty_info": "3-year warranty",
    • "end_of_contract_on": "2027-01-01",
    • "contract_notes": "Renew before expiry",
    • "create_ticket_days_before": 30,
    • "birthday": "2024-01-01",
    • "visibility": "staff_only",
    • "managed_by": {
      • "id": 10,
      • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
      },
    • "used_by": {
      • "id": 15,
      • "full_name": "Bob User"
      },
    • "accessible": true,
    • "files": [
      • {
        • "id": 42,
        • "filename": "front-panel.jpg",
        • "content_type": "image/jpeg",
        • "byte_size": 184320,
        • "created_at": "2026-06-17T10:30:00Z",
        • "url": "/api/ui/helpdesk_files/blobs/42/download"
        }
      ],
    • "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    • "updated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
    }
]

Create asset

Create a new asset.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
Request Body schema: application/json
required
asset_tag
string

Asset tag/identifier

serial_number
string

Serial number of the asset

legacy_reference_number
string

Legacy reference number

display_name
required
string

Display name of the asset

description
string

Description of the asset

asset_type_id
integer

ID of the asset type

location_id
integer

ID of the asset location

department_id
integer

ID of the department

managed_by_id
integer

ID of the user who manages the asset

used_by_id
integer

ID of the user who uses the asset

asset_status_id
integer

ID of the asset status

notes
string

Additional notes about the asset

purchased_on
string <date>

Date when the asset was purchased

cost
number <float>

Cost of the asset

purchase_order_number
string

Purchase order number

vendor
string

Vendor/supplier name

replaced_on
string <date>

Date when the asset was replaced

warranty_info
string

Warranty information

end_of_contract_on
string <date>

End date of the contract

contract_notes
string

Contract-related notes

create_ticket_days_before
integer

Days before contract end to create ticket

birthday
string <date>

Birthday/anniversary date of the asset

visibility
string
Enum: "staff_only" "all_users" "logged_in_users"

Visibility level of the asset

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/assets

Request samples

  • Payload
  • curl
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "display_name": "My asset",
  • "description": "My very own asset",
  • "asset_tag": "ASSET-001",
  • "serial_number": "SN123456789",
  • "cost": 299.99,
  • "vendor": "HP Inc."
}

  • 400
  • 401

Update asset

Update an existing asset.

PUT and PATCH are equivalent here - both apply a partial update, changing only the fields present in the request body.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the asset

Request Body schema: application/json
required
asset_tag
string

Asset tag/identifier

serial_number
string

Serial number of the asset

legacy_reference_number
string

Legacy reference number

display_name
string

Display name of the asset

description
string

Description of the asset

asset_type_id
integer

ID of the asset type

location_id
integer

ID of the asset location

department_id
integer

ID of the department

managed_by_id
integer

ID of the user who manages the asset

used_by_id
integer

ID of the user who uses the asset

asset_status_id
integer

ID of the asset status

notes
string

Additional notes about the asset

purchased_on
string <date>

Date when the asset was purchased

cost
number <float>

Cost of the asset

purchase_order_number
string

Purchase order number

vendor
string

Vendor/supplier name

replaced_on
string <date>

Date when the asset was replaced

warranty_info
string

Warranty information

end_of_contract_on
string <date>

End date of the contract

contract_notes
string

Contract-related notes

create_ticket_days_before
integer

Days before contract end to create ticket

birthday
string <date>

Birthday/anniversary date of the asset

visibility
string
Enum: "staff_only" "all_users" "logged_in_users"

Visibility level of the asset

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/assets/{id}

Destroy asset

Delete an asset. This action cannot be undone. On success the deleted asset's JSON is returned (HTTP 200).

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the asset

Responses

/api/v2/assets/{id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/assets/1?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X DELETE

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "asset_tag": "PRINT-001",
  • "serial_number": "SN123456789",
  • "legacy_reference_number": null,
  • "display_name": "Office Printer HP LaserJet",
  • "description": "My very own asset",
  • "asset_type_id": 1,
  • "location_id": 1,
  • "department_id": 1,
  • "managed_by_id": 10,
  • "used_by_id": 15,
  • "asset_status_id": 1,
  • "notes": "Located in main office",
  • "purchased_on": "2024-01-01",
  • "cost": 299.99,
  • "purchase_order_number": "PO-2024-001",
  • "vendor": "HP Inc.",
  • "replaced_on": null,
  • "warranty_info": "3-year warranty",
  • "end_of_contract_on": "2027-01-01",
  • "contract_notes": "Renew before expiry",
  • "create_ticket_days_before": 30,
  • "birthday": "2024-01-01",
  • "visibility": "staff_only",
  • "managed_by": {
    • "id": 10,
    • "full_name": "Jane Agent"
    },
  • "used_by": {
    • "id": 15,
    • "full_name": "Bob User"
    },
  • "accessible": true,
  • "files": [
    • {
      • "id": 42,
      • "filename": "front-panel.jpg",
      • "content_type": "image/jpeg",
      • "byte_size": 184320,
      • "created_at": "2026-06-17T10:30:00Z",
      • "url": "/api/ui/helpdesk_files/blobs/42/download"
      }
    ],
  • "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

Attach files to an asset

Upload one or more files (photos, documents, invoices) to an asset. Use multipart/form-data: send each file under files[]; a single file field is also accepted. On success the asset's full file list is returned (HTTP 200, not 201).

Requires the caller to be an Asset Manager (or Manager and above) with access to the asset's location. An asset can hold at most 10 files; the upload is also bounded by the account's per-file size limit and disk quota.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the asset

Request Body schema: multipart/form-data
required
files
Array of strings <binary> [ items <binary > ]

The files to upload (repeat the files[] field once per file)

file
string <binary>

A single file to upload (alternative to files[])

Responses

/api/v2/assets/{id}/files

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl -F "files[]=@/home/user/front.jpg" -F "files[]=@/home/user/manual.pdf" "https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/assets/1/files?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>" -X POST

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 42,
    • "filename": "front-panel.jpg",
    • "content_type": "image/jpeg",
    • "byte_size": 184320,
    • "created_at": "2026-06-17T10:30:00Z",
    • "url": "/api/ui/helpdesk_files/blobs/42/download"
    }
]

Delete an asset file

Remove a file from an asset. blob_id is the id from the asset's file list. On success the asset's remaining files are returned (HTTP 200).

Requires the caller to be an Asset Manager (or Manager and above) with access to the asset's location.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

ID of the asset

blob_id
required
integer

Blob id of the file to remove (the id from the file list)

Responses

/api/v2/assets/{id}/files/{blob_id}

Request samples

  • curl
  • Python
curl "https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/assets/1/files/42?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>" -X DELETE

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 42,
    • "filename": "front-panel.jpg",
    • "content_type": "image/jpeg",
    • "byte_size": 184320,
    • "created_at": "2026-06-17T10:30:00Z",
    • "url": "/api/ui/helpdesk_files/blobs/42/download"
    }
]

Search assets

Search assets using ElasticSearch query syntax. The search query format is similar to ticket search.

Search Notes:

  • Format of all date fields is: 2013-11-11T21:37:02Z
  • To search for range of date/time (i.e. for created_on field):
    • created_on:[2013-11-11T21:37:02Z TO *] (for dates after the given)
    • created_on:[* TO 2013-11-11T21:37:02Z] (for dates before the given)
    • created_on:[2013-10-11T21:37:02Z TO 2013-11-11T21:37:02Z] (for dates between the given)
  • Surround all string values with parentheses and double quotes like the following examples:
    • managed_by.email:("myemail@somedomain.com")

Searchable Fields:

  • asset_tag - Asset tag/identifier
  • display_name - Display name of the asset
  • description - Asset description
  • asset_type.id - Asset type ID
  • location.id - Location ID
  • department.id - Department ID
  • managed_by.email - Email of the user who manages the asset
  • managed_by.id - ID of the user who manages the asset
  • used_by.email - Email of the user who uses the asset
  • used_by.id - ID of the user who uses the asset
  • created_on - Creation date
  • updated_on - Last update date
  • status - Asset status
  • purchased_on - Purchase date
  • vendor - Vendor/supplier name
  • end_of_contract_on - Contract end date
  • replaced_on - Replacement date
  • serial_number - Serial number
  • birthday - Birthday/anniversary date
Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
access_key
required
string

API access key

query
string

ElasticSearch query syntax. Note the usage of %20 instead of space, & instead of just &, ( instead of (, < instead of <. Examples:

  • location.id:123 - Search all assets in certain location
  • display_name:*laptop* - Search assets with "laptop" in the name
sort_field
string
Default: "name"
Enum: "name" "tag" "asset_type_name" "location_name" "department_name" "managed_by_name" "used_by_name" "status_name" "birthday_sort" "serial_number" "created_on" "updated_on"

Sort field name (same as the web form search)

sort_order
string
Default: "desc"
Enum: "asc" "desc"

Sort order

per_page
integer [ 10 .. 100 ]
Default: 10

Results per page (min 10, max 100)

page
integer
Default: 1

Page number

Responses

/api/v2/assets/search

Request samples

  • cURL
  • cURL
  • cURL
  • Python
curl "https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/assets/search?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>&query=location.id:123"

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 403
  • 422
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 0,
    • "account_id": 0,
    • "description": "string",
    • "asset_tag": "string",
    • "display_name": "string",
    • "asset_condition": "string",
    • "serial_number": "string",
    • "checked_out": true,
    • "current_checkout": {
      • "id": 0,
      • "checked_out_to": {
        • "name": "string"
        },
      • "due_on": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
      • "is_overdue": true,
      • "is_due_soon": true
      },
    • "asset_type": { },
    • "asset_status": { },
    • "location": { },
    • "department": { }
    }
]

Asset Statuses

List asset statuses

Retrieve a list of asset statuses. Supports paging with optional parameters per_page and page. If per_page is missing, by default it will return 30 items per page.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
access_key
required
string

API access key

per_page
integer
Default: 30

Number of items per page

page
integer
Default: 1

Page number

Responses

/api/v2/asset_statuses

Request samples

  • cURL
  • cURL
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/asset_statuses?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY>

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 403
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "id": 1,
    • "name": "Active",
    • "position": 0,
    • "account_id": 1
    }
]

query Parameters
access_key
required
string

required
name
required
string

property name*
additional property
any

/api/v2/asset_statuses

application/json

Update asset status

Update an existing asset status.

PUT and PATCH are equivalent here - both apply a partial update, changing only the fields present in the request body.

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

Asset status ID

query Parameters
access_key
required
string

API access key

Request Body schema: application/json
required
name
string

Name of the asset status

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/asset_statuses/{id}

Request samples

  • Payload
  • cURL
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "name": "My precious asset type"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401

  • "id": 1,
  • "name": "Active",
  • "position": 0,
  • "account_id": 1
}

Destroy asset status

Delete an asset status by ID. Assets currently using this status are reassigned: pass move_to_status_id to move them to another status, or omit it to clear their status. On success the deleted asset status JSON is returned (HTTP 200).

Authorizations:
AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

Asset status ID

query Parameters
move_to_status_id
integer

ID of the asset status to reassign affected assets to. When omitted (or not a valid status in the account), affected assets have their status cleared.

access_key
required
string

API access key

Responses

/api/v2/asset_statuses/{id}

Request samples

  • cURL
  • Python
curl https://app.mojohelpdesk.com/api/v2/asset_statuses/1?access_key=<YOUR_API_KEY> -X DELETE

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "name": "Active",
  • "position": 0,
  • "account_id": 1
}

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
access_key
required
string

API access key

application/json

    • "id": 1,

    • "description": "My very own location",
    • "parent_id": null,
    • "position": 0
    }
]

Create location

Create a new location

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
access_key
required
string

API access key

Request Body schema: application/json
required
name
required
string

Name of the location

description
string

Description of the location

parent_id
integer

ID of the parent location (for hierarchical structure)

position
integer

Position/order of the location

property name*
additional property
any

Responses

/api/v2/locations

Request samples

  • Payload
  • cURL
  • Python
Content type
application/json
{
  • "name": "My location",
  • "description": "My very own location",
  • "parent_id": 1
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "name": "Main Office",
  • "description": "My very own location",
  • "parent_id": null,
  • "position": 0
}

Retrieve a list of locations organized in tree structure

Authorizations:
AccessKey
query Parameters
access_key
required
string

API access key

  • {

AccessKey
path Parameters
id
required
integer

Location ID

access_key
required
string

  • 200
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
Content type
application/json
